retrieved.text:
Starting with the winter semester 2022/23, you will be excluded permanently if you do not pass the STEOP. Students who have not passed the STEOP before the winter semester 2022/23 and have not resumed the degree programme by then are excluded from the associated degree programme retroactively.

Examples:

- You have not passed the STEOP in summer semester 2022: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2021/22 (or earlier) and have not resumed your degree programme: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2020/21 and have resumed your studies after one year (as possible in the old legislation) and are currently admitted: You are not affected by the expulsion.

retrieved.title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
retrieved.text:
You have started and interrupted a bachelor's or diploma programme with entrance exam/aptitude test procedure at the University of Vienna? If so, you are exempt from the entrance exam procedure for the same programme. This is also the case if there was no entrance exam procedure at the time.

**Follow these steps:**

- Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact) within the [deadline](#c4319).
- Select the topic “Bachelor/diploma programmes”.
- State which programme you would like to resume.
- Attach a scan of your valid passport or personal ID card.

i

There are **no exemptions from the entrance exam procedure** in the bachelor's programme in Psychology. Apply for admission within the [application period for degree programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/deadlines-and-dates) and participate in the written test. Resuming your studies is only possible if you are successful in the entrance exam.

An exemption is in place for certain groups of degree programmes: If you are already admitted to a programme at the University of Vienna and want to study an additional programme of or change your programme within that group you do not have to participate in the entrance exam procedure again.

This rule only applies the the University of Vienna's programmes listed below, and not to students of other universities.

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text:
You have the option of [closing your studies](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) and resuming them at a later date. Consider this option especially if it is likely that your studies will be interrupted for a long time. Please inform yourself in advance about possible conditions for [resuming your studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/). For master programmes with an admission procedure and the bachelor programme in Psychology, you must successfully complete the entrance exam/selection procedure again. For the bachelor programme in Sport Science and the teaching subject in Sports and Physical Education, you may have to take the [sporting aptitude admission test](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/zulassung-zum-studium/ergaenzungspruefung/) again. Please contact the [SSC Sport Science](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/studienservicecenter-und-studienprogrammleitung/) for more information.

As an alternative to a leave of absence, you may apply for a [remission of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). Please note the respective application deadlines and reasons for remission.

- [§ 21 Study Law of the Statutes of the University of Vienna](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/)
- [Universities Act 2002 (UG), § 67](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)

retrieved.text:
- Examination attempts in the non-degree programme also count for the degree programme. If all examination attempts are graded negatively in the non-degree programme, you are permanently excluded from the degree programme and cannot be admitted to this programme at the University of Vienna.
- You have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/), irrespective of your citizenship.
- You are not admitted to a specific degree programme, but to attending individual courses ("Besuch einzelner Lehrveranstaltungen").
- The admission to a non-degree programme is possibile until the end of the admission period. However, the registration deadlines for courses end before the beginning of the semester in some cases. Therefore, it is possible that you miss these deadlines.
- Students admitted to a degree programme are favoured in the allocation of course places. If you have any questions regarding the registration for courses and examinations, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the courses are offered.
- You are only allowed to take individual examinations/courses and must comply with the curriuclum's registration requirements. Furthermore, you are not allowed to take any public defenses. Graduating is not possible.
- There might be restrictions/limitations when it comes to health insurance, residence permit and financial assistance. Please make an inquiry at the responsible authority about the consequences of your non-degree programme up front.

retrieved.title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
retrieved.text:
Almost all bachelor’s, teacher accreditation and diploma programmes require you to complete an introductory and orientation period (STEOP) during the first semester.

The aim of the STEOP is to give you an insight into your studies and enable you to assess

- whether the course contents are in line with your personal expectations in the programme, and
- if you can meet the requirements for the degree programme.

Only after passing all STEOP examinations, you can attend additional courses and take further examinations. All examination dates are scheduled in such a way that you can continue your studies in the subsequent semester, as soon as you have successfully completed the STEOP.

You can retake examinations three times. If the fourth examination attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the examination. A resumption of this degree programme is not possible at the University of Vienna. You are also permanently excluded from all degree programmes in which this negatively assessed examimination is compulsory according to the curriculum.

You can find information about your degree programme’s STEOP and the relevant dates in [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html). Alternatively, you can also contact your [studies service center (SSC) or studies service unit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/).

reranked.title: Exemptions from the entrance exam procedure
reranked.text:
You have started and interrupted a bachelor's or diploma programme with entrance exam/aptitude test procedure at the University of Vienna? If so, you are exempt from the entrance exam procedure for the same programme. This is also the case if there was no entrance exam procedure at the time.

**Follow these steps:**

- Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact) within the [deadline](#c4319).
- Select the topic “Bachelor/diploma programmes”.
- State which programme you would like to resume.
- Attach a scan of your valid passport or personal ID card.

i

There are **no exemptions from the entrance exam procedure** in the bachelor's programme in Psychology. Apply for admission within the [application period for degree programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/deadlines-and-dates) and participate in the written test. Resuming your studies is only possible if you are successful in the entrance exam.

An exemption is in place for certain groups of degree programmes: If you are already admitted to a programme at the University of Vienna and want to study an additional programme of or change your programme within that group you do not have to participate in the entrance exam procedure again.

This rule only applies the the University of Vienna's programmes listed below, and not to students of other universities.

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text:
You have the option of [closing your studies](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) and resuming them at a later date. Consider this option especially if it is likely that your studies will be interrupted for a long time. Please inform yourself in advance about possible conditions for [resuming your studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/). For master programmes with an admission procedure and the bachelor programme in Psychology, you must successfully complete the entrance exam/selection procedure again. For the bachelor programme in Sport Science and the teaching subject in Sports and Physical Education, you may have to take the [sporting aptitude admission test](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/zulassung-zum-studium/ergaenzungspruefung/) again. Please contact the [SSC Sport Science](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/studienservicecenter-und-studienprogrammleitung/) for more information.

As an alternative to a leave of absence, you may apply for a [remission of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). Please note the respective application deadlines and reasons for remission.

- [§ 21 Study Law of the Statutes of the University of Vienna](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/)
- [Universities Act 2002 (UG), § 67](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)

reranked.title: Attending courses out of interest
reranked.text:
- Examination attempts in the non-degree programme also count for the degree programme. If all examination attempts are graded negatively in the non-degree programme, you are permanently excluded from the degree programme and cannot be admitted to this programme at the University of Vienna.
- You have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/), irrespective of your citizenship.
- You are not admitted to a specific degree programme, but to attending individual courses ("Besuch einzelner Lehrveranstaltungen").
- The admission to a non-degree programme is possibile until the end of the admission period. However, the registration deadlines for courses end before the beginning of the semester in some cases. Therefore, it is possible that you miss these deadlines.
- Students admitted to a degree programme are favoured in the allocation of course places. If you have any questions regarding the registration for courses and examinations, please contact the [StudiesServiceCenter or StudiesServiceUnit](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme in which the courses are offered.
- You are only allowed to take individual examinations/courses and must comply with the curriuclum's registration requirements. Furthermore, you are not allowed to take any public defenses. Graduating is not possible.
- There might be restrictions/limitations when it comes to health insurance, residence permit and financial assistance. Please make an inquiry at the responsible authority about the consequences of your non-degree programme up front.

reranked.title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
reranked.text:
Almost all bachelor’s, teacher accreditation and diploma programmes require you to complete an introductory and orientation period (STEOP) during the first semester.

The aim of the STEOP is to give you an insight into your studies and enable you to assess

- whether the course contents are in line with your personal expectations in the programme, and
- if you can meet the requirements for the degree programme.

Only after passing all STEOP examinations, you can attend additional courses and take further examinations. All examination dates are scheduled in such a way that you can continue your studies in the subsequent semester, as soon as you have successfully completed the STEOP.

You can retake examinations three times. If the fourth examination attempt is not successful, your admission to the associated degree programme expires on the day of the examination. A resumption of this degree programme is not possible at the University of Vienna. You are also permanently excluded from all degree programmes in which this negatively assessed examimination is compulsory according to the curriculum.

You can find information about your degree programme’s STEOP and the relevant dates in [u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/en/index.html). Alternatively, you can also contact your [studies service center (SSC) or studies service unit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/).

Each directorate of studies (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL) determines how places for courses with a limited number of participants and exams are distributed in its degree programmes. The allocation of places will take place either according to the point allocation system, the preference system or the recommended path of study. The status of your registration in u:space informs you whether you are “Registered” (Angemeldet), "On waiting list" (Auf Warteliste) or if there is an "Alternative place available" (Alternativplatz verfügbar). If an Alternative place is available, you can accept or reject it within a defined deadline. If you are registered for a course with continuous assessment and are absent from the first unit without having a good reason, you will be deregistered. Students on the waiting list will be accepted if course places become available. In case you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course, as there may be free places. Examples: Marion attends a seminar (SE) in History. Her presentation, seminar paper and contributions to the discussion of her colleagues’ presentations will be assessed. Max attends an exercise (UE) in Biology. During the exercise,

If students deregister from a course, you possibly move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. If an Alternative place is available, you can accept or reject it within a defined deadline. selected.title: Introductory and orientation period (STEOP)
selected.text:
Starting with the winter semester 2022/23, you will be excluded permanently if you do not pass the STEOP. Students who have not passed the STEOP before the winter semester 2022/23 and have not resumed the degree programme by then are excluded from the associated degree programme retroactively.

Examples:

- You have not passed the STEOP in summer semester 2022: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2021/22 (or earlier) and have not resumed your degree programme: a resumption is only possible for degree programmes, where the four times negatively assessed, examinations are not part of the curriculum.
- You have not passed the STEOP in the winter semester 2020/21 and have resumed your studies after one year (as possible in the old legislation) and are currently admitted: You are not affected by the expulsion.

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).
